name: using-xtool
description: This skill should be used when building iOS apps with xtool (Xcode-free iOS development), creating xtool projects, adding app extensions, or configuring xtool.yml. Triggers on "xtool", "SwiftPM iOS", "iOS on Linux", "iOS on Windows", "Xcode-free", "app extension", "widget extension", "share extension". Covers project setup, app extensions, and deployment.
capabilities: Create new xtool projects with proper SwiftPM structure
Configure xtool.yml files with extensions and resources
Add app extensions (widgets, share, keyboard) to iOS apps
Debug build issues and troubleshoot device connectivity
author: 2389-research
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only Markdown guidance for xtool development. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls, and no external command execution. All 67 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - bash examples in code blocks are documentation, not actual shell execution. Substring matching errors caused false positives for 'DES' in 'development', 'Path' in field names, and 'key' in Apple extension identifiers.
valueStatement: Developers who need to build iOS apps on Linux or Windows face the challenge of Xcode dependency. xtool enables Xcode-free iOS development using SwiftPM. This skill provides complete guidance for project setup, app extensions, and deployment.
userTitle: Build iOS apps without Xcode using xtool
limitations: Does not install or execute xtool commands directly
Does not modify files in your project directory
Does not build or deploy apps to devices
Requires xtool CLI to be installed separately

useCases.title: iOS on Linux or Windows
useCases.description: Build iOS apps using SwiftPM without needing macOS or Xcode

useCases.description: Add widgets, share extensions, and keyboard extensions to existing iOS projects

useCases.title: CI/CD iOS pipelines
useCases.description: Set up automated iOS builds on Linux CI runners

promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new xtool project called MyApp with SwiftPM structure

promptTemplates.title: Add widget extension
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a widget extension to my xtool iOS app called MyWidget

promptTemplates.title: Configure entitlements
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure HomeKit entitlements in my xtool app

promptTemplates.title: Debug device issues
promptTemplates.prompt: My iOS device is not recognized by xtool devices command

bestPractices: Use .library products in Package.swift for extensions, not .executable
Place extensions in Sources/ directory, following SwiftPM conventions
Keep extension Info.plists minimal with only NSExtensionPointIdentifier
antiPatterns: Using project.yml or Project.swift (xtool uses xtool.yml)
Using xtool build instead of xtool dev build

faq.question: Does xtool replace Xcode?
faq.answer: xtool replaces Xcode for building iOS apps but you still need a Mac for certain Apple services like App Store distribution.

faq.question: What SwiftPM features are supported?
faq.answer: xtool supports standard SwiftPM packages, products, targets, and resources. Most Swift packages compile without modification.

faq.question: Can I use Xcode alongside xtool?
faq.answer: Yes, xtool dev generate-xcode-project creates an Xcode project for debugging while using xtool for builds.

faq.question: Is my data safe with xtool?
faq.answer: xtool is a local CLI tool. It does not upload your code or data to any server. Authentication uses your Apple credentials only.

faq.question: First build fails or is slow
faq.answer: This is normal on first run. xtool downloads and builds Darwin SDK modules. Wait for completion and subsequent builds will be faster.

faq.question: How does xtool compare to XcodeGen?
faq.answer: xtool uses SwiftPM directly and xtool.yml configuration. XcodeGen generates Xcode projects. xtool is designed for cross-platform iOS development without macOS.
